description_long: This object consists of a group of eight irregularly shaped, faceted stones arranged in three loosely parallel rows. Each stone has a low-poly, angular appearance with flat surfaces and sharp edges, giving them a geometric, stylized look. The stones vary in size and orientation, with some stacked atop others and some lying adjacent, creating a natural yet organized cluster. The surface is uniformly gray, lacking any visible veining, mottling, or artificial carvings, and appears matte and rough to the touch. There are no visible signs of significant weathering, cracks, or biological growth, suggesting these stones are relatively unaged or recently placed. The overall arrangement suggests use as a barrier or obstacle in an urban park, sidewalk, or landscaped area.
description: A cluster of eight angular, gray stones arranged in three rows, with a rough, matte surface and a geometric, stylized form. The stones vary in size and are stacked or grouped closely, forming a naturalistic barrier.
description_view_0: From this view, the three parallel rows of stones are clearly visible, with the central row appearing tallest and most prominent.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the irregular stacking and the varying heights of the stones, with the leftmost group appearing more massive.
description_view_2: The stones are seen more from the side, emphasizing their elongated arrangement and the gaps between each row.
description_view_3: This view shows the broadest faces of the stones, with the arrangement appearing more spread out and the angular surfaces more pronounced.
attribute_placement: grouped and partially stacked as a barrier or decorative feature
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surfaces are uniformly faceted and angular with no veining or carvings.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces appear freshly cut or minimally weathered, with no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with other landscaping stones or contrasts with paved surfaces, sometimes partially embedded in soil or surrounded by low vegetation.
